what_changed: Replaced for loop with fixed 5 calls to process() from main. Demonstrates static local counter with namespace constant and shadow detection without requiring Ch 8 loops.

exercise.title: Static Call Counter with Shadowing Detection
exercise.prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete function `process` so it uses a `static` local counter to track how many times it has been called, detects a name-value coincidence (simulating a shadow scenario), and respects a namespace constant for the call limit.

## Input
- Lines 1-5: one integer value per line (the "input" passed to `process` each call).

## Output
For each call to `process(value)`, print exactly one line.

If the call count (after incrementing) does not exceed the limit `limits::max_calls` (which equals 3):
```
Call <count>: value=<value>
```
If the value equals the call count (shadow scenario), append ` [shadow warning]` to that same line:
```
Call <count>: value=<value> [shadow warning]
```
If the call count exceeds `limits::max_calls`, print:
```
Call <count>: ignored (limit reached)
```
No shadow warning is ever appended to an ignored line.

## Requirements
1. Declare `namespace limits` containing `inline constexpr int max_calls { 3 };` with external linkage.
2. Implement `void process(int value)` containing a `static int call_count { 0 };` local variable (static duration, no linkage, block scope).
3. Increment `call_count` at the start of every call, including those that exceed the limit.
4. Inside `process`, when the call is not ignored, check whether the passed-in `value` equals the current `call_count`. If so, append ` [shadow warning]`.
5. Hidden tests cover: all calls within limit with a shadow match, and all calls over limit.
6. Output must use the exact labels `Call`, `value=`, `ignored (limit reached)`, and `[shadow warning]` as shown.

test_cases.label: sample -- shadow on call 2, overflow after 3
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Call 1: value=10
Call 2: value=2 [shadow warning]
Call 3: value=30
Call 4: ignored (limit reached)
Call 5: ignored (limit reached)

test_cases.label: hidden -- all shadow warnings (value equals call index)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Call 1: value=1 [shadow warning]
Call 2: value=2 [shadow warning]
Call 3: value=3 [shadow warning]
Call 4: ignored (limit reached)
Call 5: ignored (limit reached)

test_cases.label: hidden -- no shadow warnings, all within limit negative
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Call 1: value=-5
Call 2: value=-10
Call 3: value=-15
Call 4: ignored (limit reached)
Call 5: ignored (limit reached)
